name: LoL Catalog Nexus (Tauri + Vite)
description: Offline-first League of Legends cosmetics catalog: Tauri + Rust core, Vite UI, schema-versioned local DB, and a verified prefetch pipeline—strict IPC and supply-chain aware asset handling.
longDescription: Desktop catalog for champions, skins, and chromas. Rust owns domain models, storage adapters, and Tauri commands; the frontend uses Vite + TypeScript with generated types from `lol_localdb.schema.json` so UI and persistence share one contract. Data Dragon and CommunityDragon feed prefetch scripts that hydrate `frontend/public/lol-asset-cache/` so production builds can boot without network I/O. Owned state is tracked per entity with schema migrations versioned in metadata. The architecture treats third-party CDNs as untrusted inputs: verification belongs in the prefetch tooling, not assumed at runtime.
features: Rust–Tauri IPC boundary with explicit commands instead of ad-hoc shell access
Bundled local database with schema versioning
Prefetch workflow for metadata and art assets
IPC boundary between UI and Rust services
Optional Riot API usage for rotation (non-core)
Tests and fixtures for local DB behaviour
highlights: Strict offline-first rule: no network calls when local DB exists
Asset protocol bridging for cached imagery
challenges: Large game catalogs require disciplined prefetch and cache invalidation to avoid stale art or bloated bundles.
solutions: Versioned schema, scripted prefetch, and explicit separation between bundled resources and optional live calls.
security: Treat third-party CDNs as untrusted—verify downloads in prefetch tooling
Avoid storing Riot credentials in the offline catalog; optional API is peripheral
Ship without embedding personal player account data
Schema migrations are explicit to prevent silent data corruption across upgrades
Tauri surface minimisation: only expose commands the UI truly needs
